sourceNote: Generic Life Simulator source data; source-world branding removed while mechanics are preserved.
uiRule: Gender identities are not folders under races or lineages. Use the shared genderIdentityDropdown after category/race/optional lineage selection.

races.name: Human
races.description: Humans are highly adaptable and found in nearly every environment across the world. They are known for their cultural diversity, short generational cycles, and unmatched versatility among mortal races.

races.name: Half-Elf
races.description: Half-elves are dual-natured beings born of human and elven lineage, often balancing ambition with longevity. They tend to struggle with identity but excel at diplomacy, adaptability, and bridging cultures. Their half-elf lineage dropdown now preserves the specific elvish bloodline behind the mixed heritage, including core elven lineages and Kaluseban bloodlines.

lineages.name: Half-Elf High Elf
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / High Elf. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: High Elves are scholarly and magically inclined elves who emphasize arcane study, civilization, and refined tradition. Their lineage is best represented by formal magical education, arcane cantrip traditions, and disciplined civic or scholastic culture.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Wood Elf
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Wood Elf. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Wood Elves are reclusive, nature-bound elves of forests and wildlands, swift and perceptive in living ecosystems. Their lineage emphasizes fleet movement, forest masking, practical survival, and graceful hunting among trees, undergrowth, and moonlit wilderness.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Drow (Underdark Elf)
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Drow (Underdark Elf). Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Drow are Underdark elves adapted to subterranean realms, harsh pressure, and societies shaped by secrecy or theocratic survival. Their lineage carries superior darkvision, drow magic, shadow familiarity, and instincts formed by darkness, spider symbolism, and underground danger.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Eladrin
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Eladrin. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Eladrin are fey-touched elves whose emotions and magical expression shift with seasons or states of feeling. Their lineage is defined by fey stepping, mutable grace, and close ties to the Feywild’s unstable beauty.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Astral Elf
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Astral Elf. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Astral Elves carry a starborne perspective and innate ties to cosmic or psychic magic. Their lineage expresses astral fire, starlight movement, psychic discipline, and trance practices shaped by the Astral Plane.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Sea Elf
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Sea Elf. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Sea Elves are aquatic elves adapted to life beneath waves, reefs, and sea-bound civilizations. Their lineage has swimming grace, current-reading, marine kinship, and deep bonds with ocean creatures and reef communities.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Shadar-Kai
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Shadar-Kai. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Shadar-Kai are shadow-bound elves hardened by bleak realms, despair, and entropy. Their lineage carries necrotic resilience, shadow familiarity, and a disciplined relationship with loss, memory, and the Raven Queen’s influence.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Pallid Elf
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Pallid Elf. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Pallid Elves are pale, fey-marked elves associated with luminous and uncanny wilderness. Their lineage emphasizes moonlit insight, incisive observation, curiosity, and subtle otherworldly perception.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Star Elf (Lunar/Heavenly Lineage)
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Star Elf (Lunar/Heavenly Lineage). Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Star Elves are a lunar and heavenly elven lineage touched by celestial cycles, night magic, dreams, and prophecy. Their lunar inheritance may tug at tides, omens, and emotional currents while remaining rooted in astral fire, starlight movement, and moonlit divination.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Avariel (Winged Elf)
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Avariel (Winged Elf). Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Avariel are rare winged elves whose bodies are adapted to flight, heights, and sky-bound enclaves. Their lineage emphasizes winged flight, aerial grace, wind-reading, and three-dimensional movement in mountain or aerial terrain.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Snow Elf (Frostbound Elf)
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Snow Elf (Frostbound Elf). Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Snow Elves are frostbound elves adapted to arctic, glacial, and winter wilderness. Their lineage emphasizes cold endurance, frostbound movement, calm under hardship, and ancestral memory preserved through isolation and snow.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Sun Elf
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Sun Elf. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Sun Elves are radiant, disciplined elves tied to daylight, order, and structured magical traditions. Their lineage emphasizes solar arcana, radiant discipline, heritage, perfection, and formal arcane mastery.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Moon Elf
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Moon Elf. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Moon Elves are flexible wandering elves open to other cultures, adventures, and change. Their lineage emphasizes lunar adaptability and wanderer’s grace, and moonlit magic can influence tides, moods, and travel omens when their lunar heritage matters at the table.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Shadowmoor Elf
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Shadowmoor Elf. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Shadowmoor Elves are shaped by lands of perpetual gloom and magical distortion. Their lineage emphasizes survivalist instincts, darkvision, shadow familiarity, and resilience in unstable magical environments.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Celestial Elf
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Celestial Elf. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Celestial Elves are elven people touched directly by divine or heavenly forces. Their lineage carries celestial resistance, healing light, sacred protection, and the social role of emissary, guardian, or chosen vessel.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Kaluseban
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Elf (Core Elves) / Kaluseban. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Kaluseban are elegant mana-bound people whose forms respond to ley lines, nature, moonlight, shadow, and ritual magic. As an elven-adjacent lineage in this category, they retain their own six internal bloodlines for fine-grained selection rather than being treated as a generic elf subtype.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Arcane Kaluseban
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Kaluseban / Arcane Kaluseban. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Arcane Kaluseban express luminous gold, ivory, or deep-violet beauty, glowing eyes, mana-lit hands, rune-marked garments, and scholarly ritual symmetry. They are drawn to ley lines, libraries, magical sites, diagrams, glyphs, and objects that can hold or channel spellwork. Their bloodline grants prestidigitation, detect magic, and later magic weapon or arcane lock, making them strong ritualists, warders, artificer-adjacent scholars, and arcane diplomats. They often frame Raeshkul and Sigrananna as paired forces of pattern and beauty.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Starborne Kaluseban
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Kaluseban / Starborne Kaluseban. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Starborne Kaluseban carry pale blue, silver, or amethyst skin, moonlit glow, weightless hair, celestial eyes, and an ethereal presence strongest under lunar or stellar light. They read dreams, omens, lunar phases, and celestial alignments, and their gifts include guidance, sleep, and augury. Many act as seers, dream interpreters, prophecy keepers, and guides between mortal and mystical realms. Their line often adds Sokhivar’s distant light and prophecy to Sigrananna’s moonlit grace.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Wildroot Kaluseban
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Kaluseban / Wildroot Kaluseban. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Wildroot Kaluseban have earthy tones, barklike textures, vine-wrapped limbs, leaf patterns, mosslike or grasslike hair, and eyes that echo foliage or animal markings. They are attuned to flora, fauna, green ley lines, forests, sacred trees, soil, storms, and living groves. Their bloodline grants druidcraft, speak with animals, lesser restoration, and simple emotional communication with plants and beasts. They are natural forest wardens, healers, wilderness ritualists, and protectors of the places that keep Kaluseban vitality stable.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Tideborn Kaluseban
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Kaluseban / Tideborn Kaluseban. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Tideborn Kaluseban show pearl-blue, aquamarine, dusky, or iridescent skin, underwater-current hair, deep-sea eyes, webbed fingers, and gill-like markings. They sense tides, currents, river memory, reef resonance, mist, rain, and living water, blending Sigrananna’s grace with Bastveig’s water memory. Their gifts include a swim speed, air and water breathing, shape water, fog cloud, and misty step flavored as spray or mist. They work well as river diplomats, reef envoys, tide ritualists, mist walkers, and guardians of aquatic ley places.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Stonebound Kaluseban
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Kaluseban / Stonebound Kaluseban. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Stonebound Kaluseban carry granite, sandstone, muted moonstone, or crystalline skin tones, mineral-vein hair, rune-etched markings, and grounded movement. They sense rock resonance, minerals, caverns, pressure, tremors, and subterranean sacred places, and they often honor Horundar or Anubaldir depending on whether their culture reads stone as force or burden. Their gifts include mason’s tools or Religion, mold earth, shield, and warding bond without material components. They are protective warders, cavern priests, mountain envoys, shrine guardians, and ritual architects.

lineages.name: Half-Elf Umbral Kaluseban
lineages.description: Half-elf bloodline option derived from Kaluseban / Umbral Kaluseban. Use this when a Half-Elf character carries visible, cultural, magical, or ancestral traits from this elvish bloodline while remaining under the Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage category. Source note: Umbral Kaluseban have shadowed skin, phosphorescent veins, engraved runes, shifting-symbol eyes, and clothing or movements designed for concealment. They are attuned to shadows, subterranean spaces, illusions, liminal places, forbidden transformations, and twilight bargains associated with Eirzunet’s dangerous inheritance. Their gifts include darkvision, minor illusion, disguise self, and darkness, with strong talent for stealth, observation, and subtle influence. They often become guardians of hidden paths, secret knowledge, cursed borders, or shadowed rituals where power must be handled carefully.

races.name: Half-Orc
races.description: Half-orcs are powerful hybrids of human and orc ancestry, often embodying both resilience and emotional intensity. They are frequently underestimated but possess remarkable endurance and survival instincts.

races.name: Khoravar
races.description: Khoravar are a refined mixed-heritage people often shaped by structured societies and cosmopolitan regions. They are known for blending martial discipline with intellectual or civic achievement.

races.name: Umbral Human
races.description: Umbral humans are touched by shadowed ancestry or planar darkness, giving them an eerie presence. They often possess heightened perception in low light and a natural affinity for secrecy or introspection.

races.name: Elf (Core Elves)
races.description: Elves are long-lived, graceful beings deeply attuned to magic, nature, and art. Their societies often value tradition, beauty, and refined mastery of specialized skills. They are broadly divided across many cultural and biological lineages throughout the worlds of the multiverse.

lineages.name: High Elf
lineages.description: High Elves are scholarly and magically inclined elves who emphasize arcane study, civilization, and refined tradition. Their lineage is best represented by formal magical education, arcane cantrip traditions, and disciplined civic or scholastic culture.

races.name: High Elf
races.description: Scholarly and magically inclined elves who emphasize arcane study, civilization, and refined tradition. Often associated with structured societies and formal magical education.

races.name: Wood Elf
races.description: Reclusive, nature-bound elves who live in forests and wildlands. They are swift, perceptive, and deeply attuned to natural ecosystems and survival.

races.name: Drow (Underdark Elf)
races.description: Dark-reflection elves who dwell beneath the surface world in vast subterranean realms. Often shaped by harsh theocratic societies, they are known for their affinity with shadow, spider symbolism, and survival in the Underdark.

races.name: Eladrin
races.description: Fey-touched elves whose emotions and magical expressions shift with the seasons or emotional states. They are closely tied to the Feywild and its ever-changing nature.

races.name: Astral Elf
races.description: Originating from the Astral Plane or cosmic realms beyond mortal understanding, astral elves carry a starborne perspective and innate connection to psychic and stellar magic.

races.name: Sea Elf
races.description: Aquatic elves adapted to life beneath the waves. They thrive in oceanic environments, maintaining deep cultural ties to marine ecosystems, reef civilizations, and seafaring alliances.

races.name: Shadar-Kai
races.description: Shadow-bound elves tied to bleak or shadowy realms, often serving powerful entities of the Shadowfell. They are hardened, disciplined, and resilient due to their exposure to despair and entropy.

races.name: Pallid Elf
races.description: Pale, fey-marked elves often associated with luminous or uncanny wilderness regions. They tend to be curious, observant, and subtly otherworldly in demeanor.

races.name: Star Elf (Lunar/Heavenly Lineage)
races.description: Elves touched by celestial cycles or moonlit magic, often interpreting fate and emotion through lunar phases and astral influence. They are commonly linked with prophecy, dreams, and night magic.

races.name: Avariel (Winged Elf)
races.description: Rare winged elves capable of flight, often dwelling in isolated sky-bound enclaves or mountainous aeries. They are proud, reclusive, and deeply tied to freedom and aerial mastery.

races.name: Snow Elf (Frostbound Elf)
races.description: Elves adapted to arctic or glacial regions, shaped by endurance, isolation, and survival in harsh cold environments. They often carry traditions centered on resilience and ancestral memory.

races.name: Sun Elf
races.description: Radiant and disciplined elves often associated with daylight, order, and structured magical traditions. They emphasize perfection, heritage, and arcane mastery.

races.name: Moon Elf
races.description: Flexible and wandering elves often more open to other cultures, adventure, and change. They balance arcane affinity with emotional adaptability and curiosity.

races.name: Shadowmoor Elf
races.description: Elves shaped by lands of perpetual gloom and magical distortion. They develop survivalist instincts, cautious worldviews, and resilience in unstable magical environments.

races.name: Celestial Elf
races.description: Elves touched directly by divine or heavenly forces, often serving as emissaries, guardians, or chosen vessels of higher powers. They radiate an innate sense of grace, protection, and sacred purpose.

races.name: Kaluseban
races.description: Kaluseban are elegant mana-bound humanoids whose bodies respond to ley lines, nature, moonlight, shadow, ritual magic, and bloodline affinity. They are tall and slender, with graceful features, glowing eyes, shimmering or floating hair, and skin tones that may range from pale ivory to deep sapphire, earthy green, granite, moonstone, shadow-black, pearl-blue, gold, or violet depending on lineage. They require periodic exposure to magical energy, sacred natural sites, ley crossings, celestial light, ritual practice, or enchanted environments to remain balanced and strong. Pollution, magic deprivation, or cramped urban environments can cause restlessness, diminished vitality, or weakened attunement. Their gifts include leyline sensing, subtle channeling, runic work, communion with flora or fauna, water and tide awareness, stone resonance, shadow perception, dream insight, and ritual poise against illusions or reality-distorting magic. Their culture values balance, beauty, restraint, healing, guidance, preservation, and responsibility rather than domination. Kaluseban work well as druids, wizards, clerics, warlocks, bards, rangers, sorcerers, ley-line stewards, moonlit wardens, arcane diplomats, shadow-blooded guides, and ritual arbiters.
