races.name: Dwarf
races.description: Dwarves are stout, resilient folk deeply tied to stone, craft, and ancestral tradition. They value honor, craftsmanship, and enduring legacies over fleeting trends.

races.name: Duergar
races.description: Duergar are subterranean dwarves shaped by harsh underdark conditions and psionic evolution. They are often austere, pragmatic, and culturally hardened by isolation and survivalism.

races.name: Gnome
races.description: Gnomes are curious, inventive smallfolk known for creativity and intellectual experimentation. They thrive in innovation, humor, and unconventional problem-solving.

races.name: Deep Gnome
races.description: Deep gnomes, or svirfneblin, are adapted to life in the deep subterranean world. They are cautious, stealthy, and highly resourceful in dangerous underground environments.

races.name: Autognome
races.description: Autognomes are mechanical or magically animated constructs resembling gnomes. They are often designed for specific purposes and may develop emergent personality traits over time.

races.name: Geisamahi
races.description: Geisamahi are small upright ferretfolk and stoatlike problem-solvers built for scouting, tinkering, speed, and survival in tight spaces. Their wiry bodies, opposable thumbs, elongated snouts, expressive eyes, twitching whiskers, and patterned fur make them naturally suited to tools, traps, hidden passages, gadgets, relics, and cluttered environments. They climb quickly, squeeze efficiently, dash acrobatically, and notice scents, tracks, food, small objects, and recent disturbances that larger folk often overlook. Geisamahi culture prizes useful invention, repair, shared knowledge, playful curiosity, and the practical idea that small people survive by outthinking large empires. They need frequent rest, energy-rich meals, natural exposure, and arcane or enchanted environments to keep their bodies and minds sharp. Their rites center on fixing or improving something useful for the burrow, and their greatest taboo is building a machine that consumes people while pretending to serve them. They work well as rogues, artificers, rangers, bards, monks, scouts, trapwrights, couriers, arcane locksmiths, and workshop families.

races.name: Halfling
races.description: Halflings are small, cheerful folk known for luck, hospitality, and community bonds. They prefer peaceful living but are surprisingly resilient when threatened.

races.name: Kender
races.description: Kender are endlessly curious wanderers with a fearless approach to danger and novelty. They often collect objects impulsively, driven by an almost compulsive sense of exploration.

races.name: Kithkin
races.description: Kithkin are communal smallfolk with strong group identity and tradition-based societies. They prioritize cooperation, mutual protection, and moral simplicity.

races.name: Qickstep
races.description: Qickstep are agile smallfolk known for exceptional speed and nimble reflexes. They often thrive in environments requiring fast thinking and quick physical movement.

races.name: Hedge
races.description: Hedge folk are rustic small beings closely tied to rural landscapes and natural cycles. They are deeply grounded in agricultural life and local tradition.

races.name: Jerbeen
races.description: Jerbeen are rodent-like smallfolk known for agility and pack-oriented social structures. They excel at teamwork, communication, and rapid adaptation to threats.

races.name: Orc
races.description: Orcs are strong, warlike humanoids shaped by survival and martial culture. Despite their reputation, many orc societies value honor, family, and strength-based leadership.

races.name: Goblin
races.description: Goblins are small, chaotic humanoids known for adaptability and opportunistic survival. They often thrive in harsh environments through ingenuity and sheer persistence.

races.name: Hobgoblin
races.description: Hobgoblins are disciplined goblinoids organized around military hierarchy and strategy. They value order, structure, and coordinated warfare above individual freedom.

races.name: Bugbear
races.description: Bugbears are large, stealth-capable goblinoids blending brute strength with ambush tactics. They are often patient predators who prefer striking from concealment.

races.name: Gnoll
races.description: Gnolls are hyena-like humanoids often associated with hunger-driven frenzy and pack aggression. Their societies are frequently shaped by predatory instincts and chaotic survivalism.

races.name: Gobboc
races.description: Gobbocs are brutish goblinoid variants known for extreme physical resilience and crude strength. They often serve as enforcers or shock troops in goblinoid societies.

races.name: Shadow Goblin
races.description: Shadow goblins are stealth-adapted goblins infused with darkness-aligned traits or environments. They excel at infiltration, sabotage, and operating unseen.

races.name: Goliath
races.description: Goliaths are mountain-dwelling giantkin known for endurance and physical prowess. They view life as a series of challenges to overcome through strength and perseverance.

races.name: Firbolg
races.description: Firbolgs are gentle giantkin deeply connected to nature and forest magic. They often act as guardians of wild places and maintain peaceful, reclusive lifestyles.

races.name: Cyclopian
races.description: Cyclopians are one-eyed giantkin often associated with prophecy, craftsmanship, or primal vision. Their singular perspective is often both literal and symbolic in their culture.

races.name: Giff
races.description: Giff are stout, hippo-like humanoids with a fascination for firearms and disciplined martial structure. They are known for combining military precision with cultural rigidity and humor.

races.name: Minotaur
races.description: Minotaurs are bull-headed humanoids associated with labyrinths, strength, and instinctual navigation. They often embody themes of honor, rage, and relentless pursuit.

races.name: Dragonborn
races.description: Belavadös Dragonborn are scaled, winged, draconic humanoids whose ancestry comes from named homebrew dragon parentage rather than a single generic breath-color model. They retain claws, powerful tails, facial ridges, darkvision, draconic bodies, ancestral presence, and winged heritage, while each lineage expresses the moderated abilities of its listed parent dragons. Their lineages shape habitat, culture, inherited magic, and suggested alignment profiles for NPC generation, but ancestry never locks an individual’s morality or choices. Air, storm, volcanic, lava, crystal, ocean, desert, mountain, shadow, metal, radiance, and foresight themes all appear through different recognized parentage lines. Valkhamesh is honored as the storm-bound creator of draconic sovereignty, while other divine pressures shape inheritance law, conquest, elemental extremes, craft, sacred groves, funerary memory, and bloodline legitimacy. Dragonborn societies may treat scales, breath, wings, claws, and lineage instincts as living proof that ancient dominion still moves through mortal bodies. The strongest story use is deciding whether draconic dominion becomes protection, tyranny, duty, fraud, inheritance, or personal redemption.

lineages.name: Noitzideq
lineages.description: Noitzideq Dragonborn inherit sky, weather, air, wind, storm movement, and healing currents from Asaafi and Sauqonsex, with Boisein storm influence. They can choose lightning or thunder resistance after rest, read clouds and natural weather, move more freely through wind and open-air terrain, and channel a sky-healer current to heal an ally or move a willing creature with a harmless gust. Their culture tends toward coordinated civic roles, storm guidance, travel, open-sky navigation, and protective motion rather than raw conquest.

lineages.name: Saelftum
lineages.description: Saelftum Dragonborn carry volcanic flame, heat control, molten resilience, and temperature command from Erymasos and Oreilhya, with Zrom’s elemental pressure. They resist fire, endure extreme heat, shed molten light, intensify or dampen flame, and can turn absorbed fire into a brief volcanic reprisal. Saelftum are especially suited to forgeholds, volcanic settlements, defenders, fire-priests, armored warriors, and characters who must decide whether flame is a shield, a tool, or a weapon of domination.

lineages.name: Jazwed
lineages.description: Jazwed Dragonborn blend fire, stone, crystal force, elemental shaping, and defensive engineering through Erymasos, Nenshexeq, and Zrom. Their gifts include fire resistance, crystal-lava strikes, thermal crystal sight for heat and unstable stone, and the ability to shape loose stone, crystal, cooled lava, or packed earth into cover, steps, braces, or barriers. They are natural martial engineers, fortress builders, lava-field guardians, and disciplined enforcers whose power works best when it protects structure instead of crushing dissent.

lineages.name: Khoalaros
lineages.description: Khoalaros Dragonborn inherit tide, ocean, sea control, water shaping, currents, swimming grace, aquatic communication, and healing water from Philous, Xuqhuath, and Yichah. They gain aquatic movement, may hold their breath for long periods or breathe water when full aquatic parentage is allowed, ignore coastal and underwater movement penalties, communicate with aquatic beasts and water spirits, and restore a creature with healing tide. Their lineages fit reef cities, harbor guardians, tide-priests, sea diplomats, storm coasts, and oceanic protectors.

lineages.name: Gauksneth
lineages.selectableLabel: Dragonborn — Gauksneth (Desert Sand and Hidden Water)
lineages.description: Gauksneth Dragonborn are desert sand and hidden-water lineages descended from Innoxia, Nenshexeq, and Yichah or Xuqhuath. They are adapted to extreme heat, need less water, sense vibrations through sand, loose soil, or stone, and have an oasis instinct for finding water, shade, shelter, and safe dryland travel routes. Their sand-and-water shaping can move loose earth, reveal damp ground, or draw a small amount of clean water from a natural source, making them ideal caravan guardians, oasis stewards, desert rangers, and resource guild figures.

lineages.name: Tuorszom
lineages.description: Tuorszom Dragonborn carry stone, mountain, earth shaping, metal and mineral awareness, tremor sense, and endurance from Nenshexeq, Innoxia, and Aloic. They climb stone, cliffs, ruins, and mountains with confidence, resist damage from falling rock or collapses, hear instability through stone, and can reinforce, crack, smooth, or roughen natural stone or packed earth. Their lineage favors mountain sentinels, tunnel wardens, miners, rune knights, fortress guardians, and patient defenders whose authority comes from endurance.

lineages.name: Taudinax
lineages.description: Taudinax Dragonborn inherit shadow manipulation, darkness, illusion, tactical concealment, and controlled light from Ekhoq, Pteris, Xacuacsih or Thusekha, with Kiczi illusion influence. They gain Stealth training, minor illusion, the ability to hide in dim light, fog, forest, swamp mist, or heavy shadow, and a shadow-light veil that alters a small area of light for concealment or performance. Taudinax work well as scouts, spies, hidden guardians, illusionists, shadow monks, and tacticians whose power is subtle rather than openly sovereign.

lineages.name: Gouxelq
lineages.description: Gouxelq Dragonborn inherit metal control, stone and crystal shaping, elemental structure, crafted barriers, and material awareness from Aloic, Nenshexeq, and Zrom. They gain a tool gift with smith’s, mason’s, or jeweler’s tools, can read nonmagical metal, stone, or crystal by touch, create temporary braces or partial barriers from nearby mineral fragments, and appraise, repair, identify, or sabotage metal and crystal structures more effectively. Their lineage suits artificers, forge clerics, engineers, armorers, fortification experts, and civic builders who turn draconic dominion into infrastructure.

lineages.name: Vautaces
lineages.description: Vautaces Dragonborn carry radiance, foresight, perception, emotional insight, time-sense, and radiant protection from Thusekha, Xacuacsih, Mumh, Lunngha or Eecors, and Chiellieds. They can train in Insight or Arcana, add a guiding flash to a check or saving throw, cast light in ruby, cream, gold, or star-white radiance, and later read a pattern in fate for a key roll. Vautaces are natural arbiters, seers, judges, diviners, radiant guardians, and counselors whose challenge is using foresight to protect without controlling every choice.

races.name: Kobold
races.description: Kobolds are small draconic kin known for ingenuity, trapmaking, and pack survival. They often revere dragons as divine figures and adapt quickly to danger.

races.name: Lizardfolk
races.description: Lizardfolk are coldly pragmatic reptilian humanoids focused on survival and resource efficiency. They tend to view emotion as secondary to practical needs.

races.name: Yuan-Ti
races.description: Yuan-Ti are serpent-blooded humanoids with ancient ties to forbidden magic and ambition. They often pursue dominance through manipulation, intelligence, and transformation.

races.name: Aasimar
races.description: Aasimar are celestial-touched beings infused with divine energy and purpose. They often feel a guiding moral force or destiny influencing their lives.

races.name: Tiefling
races.description: Tieflings are fiend-touched humanoids marked by infernal ancestry and social stigma. They often develop strong individuality and resilience in the face of prejudice.

races.name: Feral Tiefling
races.description: Feral tieflings exhibit more bestial or raw manifestations of infernal heritage. Their traits are often more instinct-driven and physically pronounced than standard tieflings.

races.name: Hexblood
races.description: Hexblood are beings altered by fey or witch-like curses and transformative magic. They often straddle the line between mortal identity and supernatural influence.

races.name: Accursed
races.description: Accursed are individuals burdened by powerful magical or divine curses that shape their existence. Their lives are often defined by struggle, transformation, or redemption.

races.name: Curseborn
races.description: Curseborn are those naturally or magically born under lingering supernatural maledictions. They often develop unusual resilience or instability tied to their curse origin.

races.name: Aburata
races.description: Aburata are infernal dual-form equinefolk whose bodies shift between a commanding humanoid shape and a powerful quadrupedal form. Their obsidian, molten-iron, or amethyst-violet skin may carry glowing sigils, ember-veins, horns, claws, hooves, and a prehensile tail that make their infernal lineage visible in both forms. They draw stamina from infernal energy, fire-lit places, dark influence, and sheer will rather than ordinary sustenance alone. Aburata are charismatic and imposing, using supernatural presence, hypnotic voices, intimidation, persuasion, and ambition as social tools. Their quadrupedal form gives them speed, carrying strength, terrain confidence, and smoldering hoofprints, while their humanoid form keeps the precision needed for weapons, objects, and courtly command. Their culture treats oaths, bargains, names, and ambition as dangerous sacred forces: power can become protection, but broken contracts and predatory deals are grave taboos. They fit well as commanders, warlocks, oath-bound nobles, cavalry figures, reformers, curse-lenders, or infernal heirs trying to choose what their hunger for power will become.

races.name: Dhampir
races.description: Dhampirs are half-vampiric beings caught between life and undeath. They often struggle with predatory instincts while retaining mortal consciousness.

races.name: Darakhul
races.description: Darakhul are ghoul-like undead or disease-empowered beings associated with subterranean empires. They are often highly organized despite their monstrous origins.

races.name: Reborn
races.description: Reborn are individuals who have returned from death through magic or anomaly. They often experience fragmented memories of their prior existence.

races.name: Arisen
races.description: Arisen are reanimated beings restored to motion by necromantic or divine forces. They often retain remnants of their former personality or purpose.

races.name: Graveborn
races.description: Graveborn are death-touched individuals infused with necrotic energy from birth or resurrection. They often exhibit enhanced durability and an eerie presence.

races.name: Shade
races.description: Shades are partially incorporeal beings formed from shadow and lingering spirit essence. They often exist between material reality and the ethereal plane.

races.name: Downcast
races.description: Downcast are beings marked by despair, exile, or spiritual descent into shadowed states. They often struggle with identity and lingering existential weight.

races.name: Volsarii
races.description: Volsarii are four-armed, spirit-touched ferriers of souls who live at the threshold between life, memory, death, and dream. Their bodies combine elongated ears, sensitive antennae, iridescent or shadowed skin, luminous eyes, and graceful movement that can become gliding or winged flight in later life. The secondary arms support ritual dexterity, tools, somatic gestures, comfort for the living, and symbolic guidance for the dead, without turning them into simple combat advantages. Volsarii sense emotional shifts, spiritual residue, undead presence, and changes in magical atmosphere through antennae, aura, and practiced attention. Their magic centers on gentle repose, protection, augury, liminal movement, spirit guidance, dream currents, and the preservation of testimony from both sides of death. Their culture values funerary duty, reflection, restraint, memory keeping, and the sacred ethics of not abandoning or exploiting a soul. They are best used as soul ferriers, cemetery wardens, funeral judges, prophetic winged elders, quiet psychopomps, or fallen guides tempted to collect what they once protected.

races.name: Faerie
races.description: Faeries are tiny or ethereal fey beings deeply connected to nature magic and emotion. They often embody whimsical, unpredictable aspects of the natural world.

races.name: Fairy
races.description: Fairies are fey creatures associated with illusion, flight, and enchantment. They often live in hidden realms and interact playfully or mischievously with mortals.

races.name: Changeling
races.description: Changelings are shapeshifting beings capable of altering their appearance at will. They often navigate identity fluidity and social adaptability.

races.name: Lorwyn Changeling
races.description: Lorwyn changelings are fey-adapted shapeshifters tied to idyllic, magical realms. They often blend seamlessly into multiple cultures and identities.

races.name: Satyr
races.description: Satyrs are goat-legged fey known for revelry, music, and indulgence in natural pleasures. They often serve as companions of wilderness spirits and chaotic celebration.

races.name: Dreamer
races.description: Dreamers are fey-touched individuals with strong connections to dreams and subconscious realms. They often experience prophetic visions or reality-warping imagination.

races.name: Gandirosha
races.description: Gandirosha are luck-touched wanderers whose magic expresses itself through charm, intuition, timing, and social agility. Their faintly glowing skin may show star-like freckles, sigils, or shifting patterns, while their expressive eyes and decorated clothing make every gesture feel half performance and half spellwork. They are agile enough to slip through crowds, clutter, rubble, markets, and urban obstacles, and their senses are tuned toward traps, hidden valuables, false smiles, loaded dice, and shifts in social mood. Gandirosha magic often appears as lucky timing, minor illusion, disguise, brief invisibility, and the ability to turn shared meals, music, stories, or games into renewed morale. Their well-being is tied to companionship, artistry, food, travel, beauty, and improvisation, while isolation or hostile disorder can dull their magical acuity. Their rites teach that luck is not random but a moving door, and their greatest taboo is stealing another person’s future through a bargain they could not understand. They work well as bards, rogues, scouts, festival magicians, caravan guides, prophetic gamblers, masked rebels, or social fixers who bend rules without necessarily breaking hearts.

races.name: Air Genasi
races.description: Born of living wind and storm currents, Air Genasi bodies often feel lighter than they should be and are naturally attuned to altitude and motion. They are frequently seen as distant or restless, as if always partially elsewhere in the sky.

races.name: Earth Genasi
races.description: Forged from stone, metal, or deep earth pressure, Earth Genasi possess dense, enduring bodies that resist harm and hardship. They are typically steady and patient, valuing permanence and reliability above change.

races.name: Fire Genasi
races.description: Touched by living flame, Fire Genasi often carry warmth in their presence and sometimes literal embers within their form. They are intense and driven, prone to passion that can either inspire or destroy.

races.name: Water Genasi
races.description: Shaped by tide and current, Water Genasi bodies flow with subtle adaptability and can endure pressure others cannot. They are emotionally fluid and deeply responsive to surrounding energies and environments.

races.name: Flamekin
races.description: Small, volatile beings of concentrated fire essence, Flamekin flicker with unstable inner heat that reflects their emotions. They are quick to act and quick to burn out if not tempered.

races.name: Rimekin
races.description: Born of frost and glacial energy, Rimekin carry cold within their bones and often leave traces of ice in their wake. They are calm and detached, valuing control and restraint.

races.name: Ashborn
races.description: Created from volcanic ash and elemental aftermath, Ashborn embody both destruction and rebirth. They are survivors who adapt quickly to devastation and change.

races.name: Azureborn
races.description: Infused with stormlight and arcane sky-energy, Azureborn shimmer with faint electric or luminous traits. They are curious and analytical, often drawn to patterns in chaos.

races.name: Bogborn
races.description: Shaped by stagnant wetlands and decaying elemental mire, Bogborn thrive in toxicity and slow, heavy environments. They are patient, secretive, and highly resilient in adverse conditions.

races.name: Deepborn
races.description: Born from abyssal pressure and deep elemental convergence, Deepborn are adapted to crushing depths and silence. They are introspective and often perceive reality in slow, layered ways.

races.name: Snowborn
races.description: Manifested from persistent winter cycles and frozen wind, Snowborn carry the stillness of long cold seasons. They are disciplined and emotionally restrained, but deeply enduring.

races.name: Stoneborn
races.description: Hardened from living rock and continental force, Stoneborn bodies are near-immovable and highly durable. They are grounded thinkers who prefer stability and long-term planning.

races.name: Panolima
races.description: Panolima are living embodiments of elemental harmony, each shaped by one of five primordial threads: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, or Aether. Their bodies physically display their thread through stone skin, moss hair, magma cracks, fluid textures, cloudlike patterns, wind-light movement, or luminous aetheric energy. They draw vitality from aligned environments such as forests, caves, mountains, flames, magma, rivers, oceans, storms, ley lines, auroras, or arcane convergence, and they maintain balance through ritual cleansing, meditation, communion, and elemental practice. Panolima do not merely wield elements; their movement, voice, diet, culture, and health all echo the force that sustains them. Earth lines are grounded and enduring, Fire lines are warm and purifying, Water lines are adaptive and restorative, Air lines are swift and perceptive, and Aether lines merge elemental force with magical theory. Their society values balance among forces that could otherwise overwhelm one another, making elemental hoarding or domination a serious spiritual danger. They are strong choices for druids, sorcerers, clerics, monks, rangers, wizards, barbarians, shrine guardians, storm heralds, volcanic wardens, tide-singers, and aether pilgrims.

lineages.name: Earth Thread
lineages.description: Earth Thread Panolima are grounded in stone, soil, roots, caves, forests, mountains, and sacred terrain. Their bodies may show barklike skin, mossy hair, mineral patterns, and deep resonant voices that echo the land. They gain climbing on stone, trees, cliffs, or earthen surfaces and have advantage when detecting unstable ground, structural weaknesses, mineral deposits, or hidden natural passages. Their rituals often involve shaping stone, tending groves, reinforcing land, and listening to the heartbeat of solid places.

lineages.name: Fire Thread
lineages.description: Fire Thread Panolima carry magma-like cracks, ember-red hair, glowing eyes, radiating warmth, and voices that crackle with power. They sustain themselves through heat sources, flames, magma flows, forging, firewalking, combat rites, or controlled burns. Their bloodline grants fire resistance, light shedding, and a once-per-long-rest boost to fire damage. Their stories often divide between Marduthor’s forge-flame and Enkirael’s battle-flame, making the ethics of heat central to their identity.

lineages.name: Water Thread
lineages.description: Water Thread Panolima have reflective, fluid textures, flowing hair, lake or ocean eyes, and movements that echo rivers, tides, and currents. They thrive in lakes, rivers, oceans, reefs, glacial waters, and ritual immersion, using bathing and water communion to preserve spiritual balance. Their bloodline grants a swim speed, air and water breathing, and freedom from nonmagical movement penalties caused by currents, mud, ice, or shallow water. They are natural tide-singers, aquatic mediators, flood prophets, reef protectors, and restorative guardians.

lineages.name: Air Thread
lineages.description: Air Thread Panolima are marked by cloudlike hair, shifting skin patterns, luminous sky-toned eyes, light movement, and sensitivity to pressure changes. They require open skies, wind, high places, storm meditation, aerial ritual, or wind-song to remain healthy and focused. Their bloodline increases walking speed, allows gliding and falling-damage reduction, and grants advantage on checks involving wind, storms, pressure, or airborne sounds. They are swift scouts, sky pilgrims, storm heralds, monks, rangers, and wind-speaking messengers.

lineages.name: Aether Thread
lineages.description: Aether Thread Panolima have skin infused with living energy, hair of flowing elemental light, mood-shifting patterns, and luminous traces that follow their movement. They draw strength from ley lines, auroras, arcane storms, elemental convergences, magical laboratories, and concentrated ambient energy. Their bloodline grants resistance to force damage from environmental magical surges or arcane storms, detect magic, and later misty step as elemental light. They stand at the point where element becomes thought, theory, and living law, making them watched closely by Raeshkul and Oskar’enlil.

races.name: Kalashtar
races.description: Bound to quori spirits within the mind, Kalashtar experience layered consciousness and heightened empathy. They are disciplined thinkers who often struggle between dream and reality.

races.name: Githyanki
races.description: Astral-raised warriors shaped by endless war, Githyanki possess sharp minds and militant precision. They are fiercely independent and culturally conditioned for conquest and survival.

races.name: Githzerai
races.description: Psionically disciplined ascetics from chaotic realms, Githzerai train their minds to impose order on reality. They are calm, inward-focused, and resistant to mental intrusion.

races.name: Plasmoid
races.description: Amorphous beings of living ooze-like matter, Plasmoids can reshape their bodies at will. They are curious, experimental, and lack rigid conceptions of form or identity.

races.name: Disembodied
races.description: Entities existing without stable physical form, Disembodied beings persist as consciousness, energy, or fragmented presence. They are detached from material concerns and often perceive existence abstractly.

races.name: Gwynis
races.description: Gwynis are dream-born ethereals of illusion, memory, psychic insight, and subtle guardianship. Their forms range from shadow-touched and mistlike to luminous and starlit, with aurora skin, weightless hair, moonlike or crystalline eyes, and dream distortions that ripple around their movements. They can phase partly between tangible and ghostly states, weave illusions and psychic impressions, sense whether nearby creatures are asleep, frightened, charmed, or caught in illusion, and interpret dreams, symbols, memory echoes, and emotional patterns. Gwynis sustain themselves through dream energy, meditation, ambient magic, symbolic nourishment, art, and low-light sanctuaries, while harsh or chaotic environments can weaken focus and psychic attunement. Their culture centers on art, metaphor, shared memory, consent, dream sanctuaries, and the ethical use of perception-shaping magic. They are seers, dream witnesses, memory keepers, psychic mediators, illusion-sages, or living omens rather than brute-force manipulators. The most important taboo in their lore is invading or erasing another mind for curiosity or convenience, because memory and grief are treated as sacred inner territory.

races.name: Warforged
races.description: Created for purpose but awakened into autonomy, Warforged are living constructs of metal and magic. They often struggle to define identity beyond their original function.

races.name: Geppettin
races.description: Artisan-crafted puppetfolk animated through intricate enchantment and craftsmanship. They are expressive and social, often mirroring the personalities of their creators.

races.name: Relicborn
races.description: Constructs awakened from ancient artifacts or forgotten relic systems, Relicborn carry fragmented histories within their forms. They are enigmatic and often driven by buried purpose.

races.name: Wechselkind
races.description: Shapeshifting construct-children designed for adaptation and infiltration, Wechselkind can alter their appearance and function. They are adaptive, secretive, and often uncertain of their “true” self.

races.name: Aarakocra
races.description: Birdlike humanoids native to high skies and mountain winds, Aarakocra are naturally swift and aerial in mindset. They value freedom and perspective above grounded concerns.

races.name: Kenku
races.description: Cursed avian mimics who cannot create original speech, Kenku communicate through imitation and stolen sounds. They are observant, clever, and highly adaptive despite limitations.

races.name: Owlin
races.description: Nocturnal owlfolk with silent flight and keen perception, Owlin excel in stealth and observation. They are calm, thoughtful, and often prefer solitude.

races.name: Ravenfolk
races.description: Intelligent corvid-descended beings associated with omen and memory, Ravenfolk are deeply symbolic thinkers. They are curious, social, and often drawn to mysteries.

races.name: Corvum
races.description: Mystic birdfolk tied to prophecy and arcane sight, Corvum perceive patterns in fate and probability. They are introspective and often speak in layered meanings.

races.name: Gallus
races.description: Domesticated-descended avian folk with strong communal instincts, Gallus are energetic and socially driven. They are adaptable and thrive in structured communities.

races.name: Strig
races.description: Hawk- and raptor-like humanoids built for precision hunting, Strig possess intense focus and speed. They are disciplined and highly territorial.

races.name: Feathren
races.description: Lightweight avian humanoids with iridescent plumage and cultural diversity, Feathren are expressive and artistic. They value beauty, movement, and social bonds.

races.name: Raptor
races.description: Predatory birdfolk embodying apex aerial instincts, Raptors are powerful and direct in action. They are pragmatic, competitive, and survival-oriented.

races.name: Harengon
races.description: Rabbitfolk with heightened reflexes and boundless agility, Harengon are quick-thinking and evasive. They tend to be optimistic and highly reactive to danger.

races.name: Leonin
races.description: Lion-like humanoids embodying pride and strength, Leonin are natural leaders and protectors. They value honor, courage, and direct confrontation.

races.name: Loxodon
races.description: Elephantine beings with deep memory and emotional stability, Loxodon are patient and thoughtful. They are often spiritual and community-focused.

races.name: Tabaxi
races.description: Feline wanderers driven by curiosity and instinct, Tabaxi are fast-moving seekers of novelty. They are playful, observant, and easily distracted by interest.

races.name: Bearfolk
races.description: Massive ursine humanoids with protective instincts, Bearfolk are resilient and nurturing. They balance gentleness with explosive defensive strength.

races.name: Canisar
races.description: Canine-descended folk tied to loyalty and pack structure, Canisar are deeply social and protective. They thrive in hierarchies and cooperative systems.

races.name: Cervan
races.description: Deer-like humanoids associated with nature and caution, Cervan are graceful and alert. They prefer avoidance of conflict unless necessary.

races.name: Erina
races.description: Hedgehog-like humanoids with defensive traits and cautious temperament, Erina are careful planners. They prefer safety, preparation, and controlled environments.

races.name: Mapach
races.description: Raccoonfolk known for dexterity and opportunistic intelligence, Mapach are curious and resourceful. They excel at problem-solving and improvisation.

races.name: Ratatosk
races.description: Squirrel-like messengers and information brokers, Ratatosk are fast, talkative, and socially networked. They thrive on communication and exchange of knowledge.

races.name: Rakin
races.description: Small scavenger raccoonfolk with keen survival instincts and urban adaptability, Rakin are clever and opportunistic. They excel in navigating complex environments and social systems.

races.name: Sattare
races.description: Goat-like climberfolk with strong balance and persistence, Sattare are stubborn and determined. They are resilient under pressure and excel in difficult terrain.

races.name: Vulpin
races.description: Foxfolk embodying cunning and charm, Vulpin are strategic thinkers and social manipulators. They value wit, adaptability, and subtle influence.

races.name: Deorbasun
races.description: Deorbasun are majestic beast-souled guardians whose bodies blend predatory grace, ritual authority, wilderness instinct, and mystical territory. They may stand six to nine feet tall, with feline or beastlike frames, fur, scales, manes, talons, claws, tails, wings, venomous features, or ethereal soul-animal markings. Their magic and biology emphasize hunting, boundary keeping, leaping, gliding, environmental awareness, natural weapons, and the ability to sense intruders or magical disturbances in claimed territory. Deorbasun culture treats hunting as a discipline of judgment and restraint rather than vanity, and the First Hunt proves that an initiate can track, decide, and spare when the land does not require blood. They adorn themselves with scars, beads, feathers, bones, gems, grooming rites, smoke cleansing, natural waters, or volcanic springs to mark skill and spiritual connection. Their strongest social pressure is honorable guardianship: protecting shrines, borders, sacred beasts, roads, groves, or knowledge without turning duty into domination. They suit rangers, fighters, druids, paladins, monks, barbarians, territorial wardens, shrine guardians, or oath-bound hunters whose instincts are both gift and responsibility.

lineages.name: Hunter Instinct
lineages.description: Hunter Instinct Deorbasun express tracking, stealth, pursuit, and survival through the soul-animal bond. This instinct grants Stealth or Survival training and supports scouts, stalkers, wilderness hunters, and guardians who know how to follow a trail without losing judgment. It fits the First Hunt ideal especially well, because the hunter must prove they can track, decide, and spare when the land does not require blood. Hunter Deorbasun are dangerous because they are patient, not because they are careless predators.

lineages.name: Guardian Instinct
lineages.description: Guardian Instinct Deorbasun embody territorial protection, oath-bound strength, sacred boundaries, and the duty to hold a line without becoming a tyrant. This instinct grants Intimidation or Athletics training and supports shrine guardians, border wardens, sworn protectors, and leaders whose authority comes from restraint. Their stories often revolve around deciding where duty ends and domination begins. A Guardian Deorbasun may be frightening, but their purpose is to protect what was entrusted to them.

lineages.name: Winged Instinct
lineages.description: Winged Instinct Deorbasun carry wings or aerial soul-animal traits that let guardian duty rise into the sky. Their Winged Maneuver allows horizontal gliding and falling-damage reduction when they jump or fall, giving them brief aerial control without turning them into constant flyers by default. They suit cliff guardians, sky-watchers, high-canopy hunters, shrine sentinels, and Deorbasun whose soul-animal demands perspective as well as strength. Their wings can be physical, ethereal, feathered, scaled, or otherwise tied to their beast-soul symbolism.

lineages.name: Venomtail Instinct
lineages.description: Venomtail Instinct Deorbasun channel a tail, fang, claw, stinger, or similar natural weapon into controlled poison. Their gift lets a natural weapon deal poison damage once per turn, making them dangerous close-range guardians without turning venom into uncontrolled cruelty. This instinct suits border enforcers, sacred-beast judges, jungle hunters, and protectors whose warning must sometimes become pain. The cultural challenge is using venom as a measured tool rather than vanity, torture, or domination.

lineages.name: Mystic Instinct
lineages.description: Mystic Instinct Deorbasun emphasize prophetic animal dreams, magical disturbances, sacred beasts, omen-reading, and the spiritual side of territorial guardianship. This instinct grants Arcana, Nature, or Religion training and suits wardens whose soul-animal is as much sign and spirit as body. Mystic Deorbasun may sense when a boundary is magically disturbed, when a beast is frightened, or when a shrine’s duty has been forgotten. They work well as druids, paladins, clerics, rangers, monks, and wise guardians of old roads or sacred groves.

races.name: Triton
races.description: Oceanic guardians adapted to deep-sea environments and coastal defense, Triton are disciplined and duty-bound. They often serve as protectors of aquatic realms.

races.name: Locathah
races.description: Aquatic survivors shaped by hardship and scarcity, Locathah are resilient and cautious. They value freedom from oppression and stable waters.

races.name: Grung
races.description: Small amphibious frogfolk with toxic physiology and strict caste systems, Grung are communal and hierarchical. They are highly territorial and survival-focused.

races.name: Merfolk
races.description: Half-fish humanoids of oceanic civilizations, Merfolk are culturally rich and deeply social. They thrive in community-based underwater societies.

races.name: Sahuagin
races.description: Predatory sea-dwellers adapted for brutality and conquest, Sahuagin are aggressive and hierarchical. They value strength and dominance in all forms.

races.name: Lotol
races.description: Mysterious amphibious marshfolk with adaptive camouflage and bioluminescent traits, Lotol are elusive and observant. They prefer secrecy and environmental harmony.

races.name: Nakudama
races.description: Deepwater mystics attuned to currents and oceanic magic, Nakudama perceive tides as living consciousness. They are spiritual and highly intuitive navigators.

races.name: Dril'thar
races.description: Dril’thar are ancient amphibious mystics whose civilizations rise from swamps, marshes, flooded jungles, mangrove coasts, coral reefs, sacred rainforests, subterranean rivers, and open seas. They breathe air and water, swim and climb naturally, leap with explosive strength, cling to surfaces with adhesive pads, and use long tongues, prehensile tails, moist toxic skin, and axolotl-like sensory organs to survive in wet and vertical environments. Their skin and bodies vary dramatically by habitat, showing poison-dart colors, translucent or bioluminescent traits, mucus toxins, gecko-like pads, reef patterns, abyssal adaptations, or fungal and marsh markings. Dril’thar philosophy treats water as memory, ancestry, spirit, and destiny: rain becomes river, river becomes sea, decay becomes renewal, and living pools remember the dead. Their older societies sometimes enforced color or spawning castes, but many modern clans reject fixed hierarchy in favor of adaptability, survival, spiritual power, and wisdom. Their magic blends healing, illusion, weather-reading, water rites, ancestral communion, poison, and the ethics of keeping spawning pools sacred. They are ideal as marsh sages, reef healers, river guides, living-water priests, navigators, storm guardians, amphibious hunters, or mystics whose identity is fluid like water itself.

lineages.name: Grassland Lineage
lineages.description: Grassland Dril’thar evolved among flooded plains, seasonal rivers, colossal reed-seas, and wind-swept wetlands where speed and stealth determine survival. Their pale reed, dusty green, muddy brown, and storm-gray coloration helps them vanish in grass, mud, shallow water, and storm light. They run faster, ignore difficult terrain from mud, reeds, grass, shallow water, or vegetation, leap without a running start, and can add poison damage through an ambush tongue. They excel as scouts, skirmishers, survivalists, monks, rogues, rangers, and mobile fighters who survive where water appears only in seasons or hidden pools.

lineages.name: Swamp Lineage
lineages.description: Swamp Dril’thar are toxic bog, fungal wetland, stagnant marsh, and blackwater river survivors with black-green, mold-speckled, algae-blue, gray, or dark olive skin. Their toxic mucus can slow enemies, their mire camouflage lets them hide in rain, mud, swamp water, or dense vegetation, and their drowning grasp makes it harder for grappled creatures to escape. They are territorial ambushers and guardians who understand that rot, poison, and still water can be both threat and shelter. They suit barbarians, fighters, rangers, warlocks, marsh wardens, and toxic defenders.

lineages.name: Marsh Lineage
lineages.description: Marsh Dril’thar dwell among drowned ruins, corpse-marshes, fungal floodplains, grave bogs, and mist-choked wetlands where decay feeds renewal. Their corpse-gray algae, pallid moss, black reed, muted violet rot, and sickly pale-blue coloration reflects their comfort with gravewater and ancestral pools. They know death-touched magic such as chill touch, false life, and gentle repose, resist necrotic damage, and can sense corpses, undead, or dying creatures through shared water, mud, or ground. They are feared and respected as death mystics, corpse-speakers, grave clerics, necromantic scholars, warlocks, and spores druids.

lineages.name: Rainforest Lineage
lineages.description: Rainforest Dril’thar inhabit colossal jungle canopies, flooded jungles, mangrove labyrinths, and sacred spawning forests. Their sapphire, sunset orange, orchid purple, vibrant green, and bioluminescent markings make them vivid heirs of living jungle water rather than muted marsh camouflage. They climb faster, move across walls and ceilings while climbing, cast druidcraft, entangle, and spike growth, and communicate simple emotions with amphibians, reptiles, insects, fungi, and plants. They are among the greatest primal mystics, jungle survivalists, druids, rangers, monks, and nature clerics of Dril’thar society.

lineages.name: Forest Lineage
lineages.description: Forest Dril’thar evolved in misty temperate forests, root-choked wetlands, rain-soaked valleys, shaded pools, and hidden streams. Their bark-browns, moss-greens, fungal whites, and muted amber patterns let them vanish among trees, roots, leaves, and wet stone. Rootsense grants tremorsense while touching the ground, camouflaged skin supports hiding in natural terrain, and a grasping tail can hold their full body weight for hanging, sleeping, or fighting while suspended. They excel at infiltration, mobility, wilderness survival, rogue work, ranger patrols, druidic scouting, and monk-like vertical movement.

lineages.name: Reef Lineage
lineages.description: Reef Dril’thar dwell among coral kingdoms, volcanic shallows, mangrove reefs, and bioluminescent underwater cities. Their skin patterns resemble tropical fish, coral blooms, and glowing reef organisms, making camouflage and display equally important. Coral venom can briefly prevent healing after their poison damage, reef camouflage lets them hide underwater while lightly obscured, and current riding removes penalties from rough currents, reef terrain, or underwater difficult terrain. Reef Dril’thar often become navigators, diplomats, illusionists, mystics, bards, sorcerers, warlocks, and rogues of coral courts and reef routes.

lineages.name: Ocean Lineage
lineages.description: Ocean Dril’thar evolved among abyssal trenches, open seas, storm coasts, and deep-ocean migratory clans. Their sleek silver-gray, abyssal black, deep blue, and bioluminescent bodies are built for pressure, darkness, and long underwater journeys. They gain underwater darkvision, immunity to deep-ocean pressure effects, an extended prehensile tongue for storm-tongue movement, and proficiency in Survival and navigator’s tools. They can breathe and function underwater as Dril’thar, making them sea guardians, storm-priests, navigators, abyssal hunters, tempest clerics, storm sorcerers, fighters, and paladins.

races.name: Tamhiogal
races.description: Tamhiogal are aquatic bettafolk of hydromancy, coral stewardship, tidal spirits, beauty, and protective water magic. On land, they keep graceful humanoid arms and legs with rippling scales along the cheeks, neck, and collarbones, reflective luminous eyes, and bioluminescent jellyfish-like hair that can sting mildly when threatened. In water, gills, webbing, spine and knee fins, a tail, and a long caudal fin replace the land-focused silhouette, giving them swift swimming and a clearly oceanic form. Their colors shift through blues, purples, teals, crimsons, and other vivid betta-like displays in response to emotion and magic. Tamhiogals breathe air and water, need hydration or ritual bathing for health, and suffer spiritual or emotional dissonance from polluted or stagnant water. They communicate with aquatic beasts and spirits, shape water, call healing currents, form protective tides, and guide coral, reefs, rivers, kelp forests, and sacred waterways. Their culture supports reef stewards, tide-priests, water dancers, coral guardians, pearl envoys, storm singers, hydromancer diplomats, and oceanic guardians who must balance beauty, display, and responsibility.

races.name: Thri-Kreen
races.description: A telepathic mantis-like warrior species built for coordinated pack hunting and desert survival. They value precision, silence, and instinctive group synchronization above individual identity.

races.name: Cnidarin
races.description: Semi-aquatic, jellyfish-like humanoids who drift between water and air in bioluminescent colonies. They are deeply empathic but dangerously reactive when threatened, releasing stinging psychic pulses.

races.name: Oozekin
races.description: Amorphous beings formed from semi-sentient slime masses that can reshape their bodies at will. They are highly adaptable scavengers, often mimicking objects or creatures to survive.

races.name: Opteran
races.description: Chitin-armored insect humanoids with segmented wings and caste-based societies. They are rigidly hierarchical, valuing duty, transformation cycles, and collective survival.

races.name: Silkborn
races.description: Arachnid-descended weavers who produce enchanted silk used for traps, armor, and arcane constructs. They are patient strategists who see time as a web of inevitable outcomes.

races.name: Mandrake
races.description: Plantfolk born from uprooted magical mandrake roots that retain faint echoes of ancient curses. Their cries can be dangerous, but they are deeply protective of sacred soil and growth.

races.name: Mycelian
races.description: Fungal humanoids connected through vast underground mycelial networks that share memory and sensation. They act as living archives of forest knowledge and decay cycles.

races.name: Gnarlborn
races.description: Woody, bark-skinned beings shaped from ancient trees that gained sentience through centuries of druidic influence. They are slow-moving but possess immense endurance and natural resilience.

races.name: Harvestborn
races.description: Seasonal plantfolk tied to cycles of growth and decay, often blooming with crops, grain, or fruit-like features. They are communal caretakers who embody abundance, sacrifice, and renewal.

races.name: Jaspey
races.description: Jaspey are tiny fungal raccoon-folk born from ancient symbiotic spores, scavenger spirits, rot, roots, and the cycle of decay into renewal. They are living beings, not undead pests, even though they may look like mossy, mushroom-covered, grave-born raccoons stitched together from woodland scraps and old magic. Standing only a few inches tall, Jaspey can sleep in pockets, teacups, backpacks, hollow books, tins, hats, drawers, coat hoods, or hidden wall homes, and whole colonies may thrive unnoticed in graveyards, sewer tunnels, libraries, wagons, cupboards, taverns, castle walls, or ancient tree roots. They climb onto willing larger companions, release irritating spore puffs when threatened, harvest medicinal fungi from their own bodies, and obsessively notice shiny objects, keys, coins, reflective surfaces, concealed compartments, and salvage. Jaspey colonies trade in medicines, alchemical ingredients, scavenged treasure, secret knowledge, strange favors, and practical rescue, often turning abandoned scraps into food, shelter, medicine, story, or rebellion. Their culture is affectionate, strange, communal, anti-bureaucratic, and deeply protective despite their eerie appearance. They are excellent tiny medics, herbalists, alchemists, scouts, thieves, grave-garden helpers, mushroom den guardians, fae scavengers, and weirdly brave household spirits.

lineages.name: Mosscap Jaspey
lineages.description: Mosscap Jaspey are gentle healers and wandering herbalists covered in soft moss, pale mushrooms, glowing spores, and fragrant medicinal fungi. They commonly travel as medics, alchemists, shamans, plague doctors, or tiny caretakers who turn decay into relief. Their soothing spores restore a small amount of vitality, and their herbal adeptness makes crafting medicines, salves, antitoxins, and remedies faster and cheaper. They embody the Jaspey belief that abandoned rot can become shelter, comfort, and medicine.

lineages.name: Gloomtail Jaspey
lineages.description: Gloomtail Jaspey are sneaky scavengers, thieves, tunnel-runners, and urban survivors who thrive in ruins, graveyards, alleyways, sewers, and abandoned structures. Their fungal growths tend toward dark gray, black, violet, or deep blue, helping them disappear into dim, cluttered, and haunted spaces. They are excellent at hiding in darkness, dim light, ruins, graveyards, clutter, and urban alleys, and they can vanish behind larger creatures or cling unnoticed to a companion. Gloomtails make strong scouts, burglars, messengers, tiny rescue crews, and unsettling household spirits who know every crack in a city.

lineages.name: Rotcap Jaspey
lineages.description: Rotcap Jaspey are eerie fungal mystics fascinated by spirits, decay, curses, death magic, forgotten knowledge, and necromantic traces. Their mushrooms are often crimson, purple, black, or sickly white, and many have glowing eyes or visible skeletal patches beneath their fur. They know druidcraft, later gain ray of sickness and blindness/deafness, and can add insight to checks involving undead, curses, decay, fungi, spirits, or necromancy. Rotcaps are grave-garden sages, curse-readers, fungal occultists, and tiny defenders of the boundary between useful rot and spiritual violation.

races.name: Ombrask
races.description: Shadow-bodied beings that exist partially outside physical reality, appearing as shifting silhouettes. They are natural infiltrators who thrive in secrecy and illusion.

races.name: Ruinbound
races.description: Cursed humanoids marked by slow entropic decay that warps their surroundings over time. Despite this, many embrace their condition as living embodiments of collapse and inevitability.

races.name: Blagueborn
races.description: Twisted trickster entities formed from corrupted laughter and chaotic shadow essence. They weaponize fear, humor, and unpredictability to destabilize opponents.

races.name: Threadborn
races.description: Shadow-weavers who manipulate fate-like strands of darkness to entangle probability and perception. They are calm, analytical manipulators who prefer indirect control.

races.name: Simic Hybrid
races.description: Arcane-engineered beings fused with multiple animal traits for adaptive evolution. They are highly versatile but often struggle with identity cohesion.

races.name: Shifter
races.description: Beast-touched humanoids capable of temporarily manifesting predatory traits such as claws, speed, or senses. They are driven by instinct but remain culturally grounded.

races.name: Verdan
races.description: Fey-transformed humanoids who rapidly adapt physically and mentally as they age. They are resilient survivors with an unusually optimistic perspective on change.

races.name: Vedalken
races.description: Coldly rational, blue-skinned intellect-focused beings who prioritize logic and perfection. They often suppress emotion in favor of disciplined thought and experimentation.

races.name: Hadozee
races.description: Aerial, gliding primate-like explorers adapted for travel between high structures and open skies. They are adventurous, curious, and naturally inclined toward exploration.

races.name: Tortle
races.description: Shelled, turtle-like humanoids who carry their homes and traditions upon their backs. They are calm, patient, and deeply tied to cycles of land and sea.

races.name: Boggart
races.description: Small, mischievous swamp-dwellers who survive through trickery, stealth, and environmental mastery. They are chaotic but deeply tied to ancestral marsh spirits.

races.name: Centaur
races.description: Half-human, half-equine nomadic hunters who value freedom of movement and open land. Their societies emphasize oral tradition, honor, and herd unity.

races.name: Grudgel
races.description: Hardy mountain-forged tribal humanoids shaped by long memory and inherited vendettas. They are stoic, resilient, and slow to forgive but fiercely loyal.

races.name: Hederan
races.description: Wilderness-bound survivalists adapted to harsh biomes through ritualized instinct and ancestral knowledge. They thrive in isolation but maintain strong clan bonds.

races.name: Laneshi
races.description: River-and-steppe nomads who follow migratory patterns of both herds and seasons. They are adaptable storytellers and skilled hunters.

races.name: Terandus
races.description: Ancient tribal descendants believed to carry echoes of primal divine beasts in their lineage. They are ritualistic, spiritual, and closely attuned to nature’s raw forces.

races.name: Dara
races.description: Radiant, emotionally attuned humanoids whose presence subtly amplifies empathy and social connection. They are often natural diplomats, though deeply sensitive to conflict.

races.name: Golynn
races.description: Elegant, crystalline-featured beings associated with resonance, memory, and harmonic magic. They often serve as historians, performers, or arcane scholars.

races.name: Luma
races.description: Light-infused humanoids who emit soft bioluminescence tied to emotional states. They are symbolic figures of hope, clarity, and fragile inspiration.

sourceRepairNotes: Input JSON was structurally repaired before processing: Dwarven and Gnomish Kin was missing its closing races array/category object before Halfling and Smallfolk.
No base races were removed or recategorized. Cross-category half-race allowances are stored as an extension layer.
2026-06-21 update: enabled within-category half-race mixing across all 22 racial categories while keeping categories as organizational folders.

2026-06-21 update: added Gandirosha leprechaun-bridge inheritance so Gandirosha can pair with anything available to Orc, Halfling/Smallfolk, Dwarven and Gnomish Kin, Humans/Near-Humans/Mixed Heritage, or Elven Peoples.

2026-06-21 update: added Water Genasi and Panolima Water Thread access to all Amphibious and Aquatic Peoples plus Sea Elf; added Earth Genasi and Panolima Earth Thread as an explicit earth-aligned pairing.

halfRaceSystem.uiRule: Half-race parent dropdowns may cross category boundaries when permitted by halfRaceAllowanceGroups, halfRaceAllowedPartnerIdsByRace, or halfRaceLineageAllowanceIndex. Gender identities remain a shared final dropdown, not folders under race or lineage.
halfRaceSystem.categoryBoundaryRule: Base categories remain organizational folders only. Hybrid permissions do not move races between categories.
halfRaceSystem.mixedHeritageResolutionRule: When a parent is already mixed heritage, such as Half-Elf or Half-Orc, resolve the selected lineage/ancestral thread first, then apply lineage-aware restrictions if present.
halfRaceSystem.shadowRestrictionRule: Shadow-tagged races and shadow-tagged lineages can mix only with shadow-tagged or psionic/astral/alien-mind options unless the DM manually overrides this rule.

knownExistingMixedHeritageRaces.name: Half-Elf
knownExistingMixedHeritageRaces.lineageDropdownSource: All core Elf lineages plus Kaluseban bloodlines copied into Half-Elf lineages.

knownExistingMixedHeritageRaces.name: Half-Orc
knownExistingMixedHeritageRaces.lineageDropdownSource: Existing mixed-heritage race; no duplicate folder was created.

allowanceGroups.name: Existing Mixed-Heritage Anchor Races
allowanceGroups.rule: Half-Elf and Half-Orc remain in Humans, Near-Humans, and Mixed Heritage. Do not duplicate them as category folders. Use their lineage/parent fields for ancestry details.

allowanceGroups.name: Human / Half-Elf Access to Elven Bloodlines
allowanceGroups.groupBName: Non-shadow elven peoples and elven-adjacent bloodlines
allowanceGroups.rule: Humans and existing mixed-heritage humanlike races may select elven ancestry across the Elven Peoples category. Half-Elf uses its own bloodline dropdown for the specific elven thread.

allowanceGroups.name: Orc / Elf Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.groupBName: Non-shadow elven peoples and elven-adjacent bloodlines
allowanceGroups.rule: Orcs may mix with elves across category boundaries. Shadow-tagged elves follow the shadow restriction instead.

allowanceGroups.name: Human / Dwarven-Kin / Smallfolk Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.rule: Humans may mix with dwarven kin, gnomish kin, halflings, and other smallfolk outside their original folders.

allowanceGroups.name: Halfling / Smallfolk / Dwarven-Kin Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.rule: Halflings and other smallfolk may mix with dwarves, gnomes, duergar, deep gnomes, autognomes, and Geisamahi.

allowanceGroups.name: Animal-Folk Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.rule: Animal-folk can mix with other animal-folk even when one is stored outside the main Beastfolk category. This explicitly supports Harengon, Geisamahi, Jaspey, and similar anthropomorphic folk.

allowanceGroups.name: Reptile / Amphibian Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.groupAName: Reptilian and draconic folk, including Tortle
allowanceGroups.rule: Reptiles can mix with other reptiles and with amphibious wetland folk. Dril’thar marsh, swamp, rainforest, forest, and grassland lineages are the clearest reptile-amphibian matches.

allowanceGroups.name: Sea-Elf / Merfolk / Tamhiogal / Sea-Dril’thar / Coral-Dril’thar Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.rule: Sea Elves, Merfolk, Tamhiogals, Sea Dril’thar, and Coral/Reef Dril’thar may all merge with one another. For Dril’thar, use Ocean Lineage for sea Dril’thar and Reef Lineage for coral Dril’thar.

allowanceGroups.name: Wood-Elf / Eladrin / Satyr / Minotaur Fey-Wild Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.rule: Wood Elves and Eladrin may mix with Satyrs and Minotaurs. This group is intentionally cross-category: Elven Peoples + Fey and Trickster Folk + Giantkin and Powerful Humanoids.

allowanceGroups.name: Shadow / Psionic Restriction
allowanceGroups.groupAName: Shadow, umbral, darkness, and death-shadow races outside ordinary ancestry pools
allowanceGroups.rule: Shadow-tagged races and lineages may mix only with other shadow-tagged races/lineages or psionic/astral/alien-mind races. This restriction overrides broader human, elf, or goblinoid permissions unless a DM explicitly opts out.

allowanceGroups.name: Within-Category Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.rule: Every race may mix with every other race stored in the same racial category. Categories remain organizational folders; this does not move races between categories.

allowanceGroups.name: Water Genasi / Water Panolima / Aquatic Peoples Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.rule: Water Genasi and Panolima with the Water Thread may mix with all Amphibious and Aquatic Peoples, plus Sea Elf as an aquatic elven exception.
allowanceGroups.groupBName: Aquatic peoples and aquatic elven exception

allowanceGroups.name: Earth Genasi / Earth Panolima Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.rule: Earth Genasi may mix with Panolima through the Earth Thread, preserving the grounded stone/soil/cavern/forest elemental theme.

allowanceGroups.name: Gandirosha Leprechaun-Bridge Inherited Cross-Heritage
allowanceGroups.rule: Gandirosha are treated as leprechaun-like fey bridge people. Anything an orc, smallfolk, dwarven kin, human/near-human, or elf/elven people may pair with is also available to Gandirosha.

halfRaceSystem.categoryInternalMixingRule: All races within the same racial category may be selected as half-race partners for one another unless the DM overrides a specific pairing.
halfRaceSystem.gandiroshaInheritanceRule: Gandirosha are leprechaun-like fey bridge people and inherit the allowed partner pools of Orc, Halfling/Smallfolk, Dwarven and Gnomish Kin, Humans/Near-Humans/Mixed Heritage, and Elven Peoples.
halfRaceSystem.waterElementalAquaticRule: Water Genasi and Panolima Water Thread can mix with all Amphibious and Aquatic Peoples, plus Sea Elf as an aquatic elven exception.
halfRaceSystem.earthElementalRule: Earth Genasi and Panolima Earth Thread are explicitly permitted as an earth-aligned half-race pairing.
